summary: Mechanical cleanup PR that ran the repository's `fix-labels` tooling across assembly sources. The visible effect was converting many local data labels from exported/function-style names such as `ftCo_804D3C78` or `ftCo_804D8DC8` to local `.L_...` labels and updating all same-file references, including `@sda21` small-data loads and `.4byte` pointers. The largest impact was in fighter assembly files, especially `asm/melee/ft/ft_0D14.s`, `ftchangeparam.s`, `ft_0C88.s`, and `ft_0C31.s`. `Cargo.lock` also changed because `proc-macro2` was updated from 1.0.76 to 1.0.78. There were no PR body notes, issue comments, review comments, or review records in the provided slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred only from the mechanical label changes.
what_changed: Ran `fix-labels` over 18 files, producing a balanced mechanical diff of 1212 added and 1212 deleted lines.
Renamed local assembly labels that had subsystem-prefixed symbol names to `.L_` local-label form, for example `ftCo_804D3B48` became `.L_804D3B48` in `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09F4.s`.
Updated references to renamed labels in instructions and data initializers, for example `.4byte ftCo_804D3B48` became `.4byte .L_804D3B48`.

Updated small-data references to renamed local labels, for example `addi r3, r13, ftCo_804D3C78@sda21` became `addi r3, r13, .L_804D3C78@sda21`, and `lfs f1, ftCo_804D8DC8@sda21(r2)` became `lfs f1, .L_804D8DC8@sda21(r2)`.

Touched many fighter assembly routines in the excerpt, including code around `ftCo_800C61B0`, `ftCo_800C6408`, `ftCo_EntryStart_Phys`, `ftCo_EntryStart_Coll`, `ftCo_EntryEnd_Phys`, and `ftCo_EntryEnd_Coll`, but the excerpt shows label-reference cleanup rather than behavioral instruction changes.
Updated `Cargo.lock` by changing `proc-macro2` from version `1.0.76` to `1.0.78` with its corresponding checksum.
smart_moves: Kept the change mechanical and balanced: the added/deleted line counts match, indicating label text substitutions without intended code-size or logic changes.
Updated both label definitions and every visible reference form, including `.4byte`, `addi ... @sda21`, `lfs ... @sda21(r2)`, and `lfd ... @sda21(r2)`, avoiding dangling assembly symbol references.
Used `.L_` for labels that appear to be local constants or local data rather than globally meaningful functions, reducing symbol namespace pollution and making future decomp symbol discovery cleaner.
Grouped the cleanup in a dedicated PR titled for the tool run, making it easier to separate mechanical label churn from semantic decomp work in later archaeology.
decomp_lessons: When converting assembly labels, update every use site, not just the definition; PowerPC small-data relocations such as `@sda21` must refer to the new label spelling exactly.
Labels representing local constants, assertion strings, or file-local data should prefer `.L_<address>` form unless evidence supports a named global symbol.
Large label-normalization diffs are best done as isolated tool-generated PRs so future matching PRs are not obscured by unrelated assembly label churn.
A balanced add/delete diff in assembly can still affect buildability if any relocation reference is missed; validate generated label changes with assembler/build checks.
Do not infer new function names from address-like local data labels solely because a previous label used a subsystem prefix; this PR demonstrates demoting such names back to `.L_` local labels.
naming_conventions: Use `.L_<address>` for local assembly labels, e.g. `.L_804D3B48`, `.L_804D3C78`, `.L_804D8DC8`, `.L_804D8DE0`.
Reserve subsystem-prefixed names like `ftCo_800C61B0` for actual global functions or symbols that remain declared as labels without the `.L_` local prefix.
For address-derived local constants in small-data sections, keep the address suffix but make locality explicit with `.L_`.
Global data labels that are still exported remain marked with `.global`, as shown by `ftCo_804D3B50` and `ftCo_804D3B58` in the excerpt, while the pointed-to local label was demoted to `.L_804D3B48`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Apply label renames uniformly across definition sites, `.4byte` data references, and instruction operands.
Preserve relocation suffixes such as `@sda21` and addressing modes such as `@sda21(r2)` or `@sda21` while changing only the symbol name.
Keep address comments and instruction bytes unchanged during label normalization to preserve assembly auditability.
Use local `.L_` labels to reduce accidental cross-file symbol collisions and to avoid presenting anonymous constants as decompiled named globals.
Treat fix-labels output as a mechanical pre-clean step before decompiling or matching functions that reference these constants.
review_feedback: No review comments, issue comments, review records, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested follow-up was available; conclusions are based on the PR title and diff excerpt only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that depend on `.L_804D3C78` or `.L_804D8DC8` after fix-labels cleanup.
Search for remaining non-`.L_` address-derived labels in `asm/melee/ft` that may need another `fix-labels` pass.
Check whether the `proc-macro2` Cargo.lock update was incidental to running the tooling or required by the fix-labels toolchain.
Compare subsequent decomp PRs touching `ft_0D14.s`, `ftchangeparam.s`, `ft_0C88.s`, or `ft_0C31.s` for reduced label noise.
